beforeEnrollmentChecklist.title: The requirements for geography majors with the option in data science are as follows

requirementGroup.label: The requirements for geography majors with the option in data science are as follows
requirementGroup.sourceHeading: The requirements for geography majors with the option in data science are as follows:: One from CSE 180, INFO 201, GEOG 245, or GEOG 258 Quantitative Methods: One from GEOG 317, GEOG 326, GEOG 426, STAT 220, STAT 221, STAT 290, STAT 311, STAT 390, Q SCI 381, or QMETH 201 GEOG 360: Introduction to GIS & Mapping GEOG 315: Explanation and Understanding in Geography (5 credits). This course covers the beginning steps in the research process. Students develop basic library and writing skills as preparation for future research methods classes and independent research. Recommended preparation includes 10-15 credits of prior GEOG curriculum coursework.
requirementGroup.sourceRowText: The requirements for geography majors with the option in data science are as follows:: One from CSE 180, INFO 201, GEOG 245, or GEOG 258 Quantitative Methods: One from GEOG 317, GEOG 326, GEOG 426, STAT 220, STAT 221, STAT 290, STAT 311, STAT 390, Q SCI 381, or QMETH 201 GEOG 360: Introduction to GIS & Mapping GEOG 315: Explanation and Understanding in Geography (5 credits). This course covers the beginning steps in the research process. Students develop basic library and writing skills as preparation for future research methods classes and independent research. Recommended preparation includes 10-15 credits of prior GEOG curriculum coursework.

uw-seattle-geography.recommendedTrackSummary: AAS-T is the current closest Green River transfer path for this degree because it matches 2 of the 2 degree-specific Green River classes currently tracked for this major.
whyThisTrack: AAS-T has the strongest direct overlap with the current degree-specific Green River class list, including MATH& 146, CS 122.

This auto-match compares every hardcoded course in the current Green River transfer tracks against the major's tracked Green River classes and keeps the track with the highest concrete course overlap.
Use the remaining major-specific checklist items to add the classes that AAS-T does not cover by itself.

Recommended preparation includes 10-15 credits of prior GEOG curriculum coursework.
options.label: GEOG 326

uw-seattle-geography.recommendedTrackSummary: AAS-T is the current closest Green River transfer path for this degree because it matches 3 of the 6 degree-specific Green River classes currently tracked for this major.
whyThisTrack: AAS-T has the strongest direct overlap with the current degree-specific Green River class list, including MATH& 146, CS 122, CS 123.

This auto-match compares every hardcoded course in the current Green River transfer tracks against the major's tracked Green River classes and keeps the track with the highest concrete course overlap.
Use the remaining major-specific checklist items to add the classes that AAS-T does not cover by itself.

uw-seattle-geography.recommendedTrackSummary: AAS-T is the current closest Green River transfer path for this degree because it matches 3 of the 6 degree-specific Green River classes currently tracked for this major.
whyThisTrack: AAS-T has the strongest direct overlap with the current degree-specific Green River class list, including MATH& 146, CS 122, CS 123.

This auto-match compares every hardcoded course in the current Green River transfer tracks against the major's tracked Green River classes and keeps the track with the highest concrete course overlap.
Use the remaining major-specific checklist items to add the classes that AAS-T does not cover by itself.
